title: Rose and oud solid perfume
subtitle: A beeswax and jojoba solid perfume with rose and oud fragrance oils, set in a small tin
excerpt: Solid perfume is a beeswax-and-oil base holding a high concentration of fragrance. It melts on contact with skin warmth, releasing the scent gradually. Rose and oud is a classic oriental pairing: the rose provides sweet floral top notes and the oud provides deep woody base. The final fragrance concentration is around 25%, which is at the high end for a leave-on product.
sourceNotes: Solid perfume base: beeswax 33%, jojoba oil 62%, fragrance oils 5% total. Fragrance load for solid perfume typically 15 to 30%; 25% is achievable with fragrance oils (not pure essential oils). Rose fragrance oil and oud fragrance oil in 3:1 ratio. Jojoba as carrier: very stable, 2+ year shelf life, suitable for skin. Pour into a 15 ml or 20 ml metal tin.

recipe.yieldDescription: ~15 g solid perfume (fills a 15 ml round tin)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep beeswax at 33% for a firm solid perfume at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 1 hour to set fully before use.
recipe.temperatureNote: Melt beeswax to 65 to 70°C. Remove from heat and allow to cool to 50°C before adding fragrance oils.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 2 years in a sealed tin at room temperature. Beeswax and jojoba are both very stable. The fragrance may mellow over time. Store away from direct heat.

glossaryTerms.definition: A wax-based perfume that is solid at room temperature and melts on contact with skin warmth. The fragrance is released gradually as the wax softens. Higher fragrance concentrations (15 to 30%) are possible in a solid base compared to a body lotion.

A rich, dark, resinous fragrance derived from agarwood (Aquilaria spp). One of the most expensive natural fragrance materials. In a blend it functions as a deep, tenacious base note. Oud fragrance oil is a synthetic approximation widely used in home fragrance.

content.text: carries fragrance well above the concentration achievable in a lotion or spray. Rose fragrance oil provides the sweet floral character;

grounds it with a deep woody base. Fragrance oils go in after the beeswax cools to 50°C; adding them to hot wax drives off the volatile top notes before the perfume has set. Never leave melting beeswax unattended. Keep a kitchen-fire blanket within reach.

items.prepNote: 33%; provides firm set at room temperature
Rose fragrance oil; 3:1 ratio with oud; add at 50°C

content.text: Melt the beeswax in the beaker over a simmering-water bath to 65 to 70°C. Remove from heat. Add the jojoba oil and stir to combine. Allow to cool to 50°C (check with probe thermometer). Add both fragrance oils and stir for 1 minute.
Pour immediately into the tin. Leave undisturbed at room temperature for 1 hour to set. The surface should be smooth and firm.
Dab a fingertip onto the solid perfume and apply to pulse points: wrists, inner elbows, base of the neck. The warmth of the skin melts the wax and releases the fragrance.
2 years in a sealed tin away from direct heat. Keep the lid on to slow fragrance loss.
